relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Joab reads the king's unresolved longing and turns it into a political project.
A wise woman is asked to wear another woman's grief as a costume.
The commander supplies the script she will carry into the throne room.
She enters royal space through humility and a cry for rescue.
The invented widow begins with the vulnerability of bereavement.
Her story places one surviving son under threat after brother kills brother.
Clan retaliation would erase the widow's last relationship, heir, and social future.
David promises to intervene.
She offers to carry guilt so the king will act without fear for his throne.
The king extends protection against further intimidation.
She presses for a sworn boundary against the avenger of blood.
Having secured the principle, she asks permission to turn it toward the king.
She accuses David of condemning himself by leaving his own banished son outside.
Her water image names mortality and imagines Divine work that creates a path home.
The fictional story continues long enough to protect the real political appeal.
She repeats the widow's need for rescue from annihilation.
Flattery presents David as a nearly angelic judge, showing what vulnerable petitioners may say to power.
David begins testing the hidden structure behind the speech.
The woman admits that Joab commissioned and scripted the encounter.
She praises David's perception while explaining the rhetorical disguise.
David authorizes Absalom's return.
Joab celebrates success in gaining the king's favor.
The exiled prince is brought back to Jerusalem.
David permits geographic return but withholds relationship and hearing.
Public admiration fixes upon Absalom's appearance as though beauty were political substance.
Even his hair becomes measurable royal spectacle.
He gives his daughter the name Tamar, carrying his sister's name into another generation.
Two years of unresolved exclusion pass inside the capital.
Absalom's repeated attempts to reach Joab are ignored.
He answers blocked communication by burning Joab's barley field.
The property damage finally produces the meeting ordinary requests could not obtain.
Absalom demands either access to judgment or a clear sentence rather than indefinite limbo.
A royal kiss closes the scene, but unresolved violence remains beneath the gesture.

contemplative_note: Reconciliation without truth is theater. The wise woman's speech opens a path home, yet no one asks what Tamar needs, what justice requires, or whether restored proximity is safe. The chapter warns against beautiful appearances, scripted mediation, and kisses that cover unaddressed harm.
